brief_justification: Correct answer (Yes) with valid causal reasoning and exact numerical computation; Multi-level analysis covering causal graph structure, backdoor criterion, do-calculus identification, and baseline vs intervention comparison; Explicit suppression of observational-to-causal leaps shows some metacognitive awareness but no mid-course correction needed; Verified by checking backdoor paths, computing baseline probability, and comparing pre/post intervention values from different angles; Clearly states why observational equals interventional (S is root node, no confounders) and identifies the structural basis for confidence; Mentions backdoor paths and confounding as alternatives to consider but doesn't deeply explore competing interpretations; Complete derivation chain from graph structure through do-calculus to final numerical comparison, fully reproducible
